pattern: Because every value is small, tracking the full SET of achievable OR-results for choosing exactly j elements from a prefix (and separately from a suffix) lets every split point be combined by brute-force XOR over two small sets.
hints: Values are small (well under 128), so instead of tracking one best OR value per prefix, track the entire set of OR values achievable by choosing exactly j elements from the first i elements -- there are at most ~128 distinct values in that set.

Build this achievable-set table from the left (prefix, exactly k elements chosen) and independently from the right (suffix, exactly k elements chosen); each new element either gets skipped or OR'd into one more chosen slot.
For every split point between the first k picks and the last k picks, XOR every achievable prefix-OR value against every achievable suffix-OR value and keep the largest result.

edge_inputs.description: all identical values, OR and XOR collapse
greedily taking the two largest values on each side is not optimal for XOR
k spans exactly half the array, forcing one specific split point
powers of two so OR combinations are easy to reason about but XOR still needs the full search

stress.description: random values within the small range, random valid k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(60, size))
    if n % 2 == 1:
        n -= 1
        n = max(2, n)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 127) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n // 2)
    return [nums, k]
